header.label: HOME
header.title: Dídac Llorens
header.subtitle: Software engineer in Barcelona working on agentic workflows, developer tooling, technical documentation, and backend/data systems.

blocks.alt: 418 banner artwork with Dídac Llorens visual identity

blocks.lead: I work on software projects where workflow, implementation, documentation, and review need to stay aligned.
paragraphs: My current focus is agentic workflow engineering, developer tooling, static publishing, LaTeX/report systems, and public technical artifacts. My background combines banking and insurance operations, Java/Spring backend development, ML/AI studies, and formal technical documentation.

items.value: Agentic workflows, developer tooling, static publishing, and technical documentation
Java/Spring backend work, ML/AI studies, banking and insurance operations
AI workflow engineering, research engineering, ML/data systems with domain depth, and backend/platform work with real technical constraints

paragraphs: This site is built from static HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and JSON content files. The repository contains the public pages, reports, CV material, project notes, and workflow state.
The content model keeps visible copy in data and reusable fragments, while the shell and renderer stay generic.

header.title: ML/data systems
header.subtitle: Data flows, automation, validation, model-adjacent work, and domain constraints.

paragraphs: This view is for ML/data conversations where the relevant work is the system around the data: flows, model behavior, automation, validation, documentation, and constraints.
Banking and insurance operations shaped how I read data work: process, risk, responsibility, and context are part of the system.

header.label: FOCUS / AGENTIC
header.title: Agentic systems
header.subtitle: Scoped automation, file-based state, review gates, technical documentation, and developer tooling.

paragraphs: My work with agentic systems focuses on orchestration: context boundaries, file-based state, review gates, logs, tests, and human approval where outputs affect public or repository surfaces.
AgenticCareerBoost is the main artifact for this view. It turns those rules into source files, public pages, reports, state updates, validation checks, and pull requests.

items.title: Agentic CV view
items.text: Tooling, documentation, static publishing, and reviewable outputs.

items.title: AgenticCareerBoost
items.text: The main project behind this angle.

items.title: Agents need receipts
items.text: A note on agentic work and project memory.

header.label: FOCUS / BACKEND
header.title: Backend and tooling
header.subtitle: Java/Spring base, domain rules, technical documentation, and platform-oriented work.

paragraphs: This view is for backend/platform conversations about behavior, boundaries, tooling, and maintainability. Java/Spring is part of the route, not the whole identity.
I am most interested in backend work when the domain has real shape: rules, transitions, permissions, failures, and enough complexity to reward clear structure.

IronBank shows the older backend thread. P3CTeX shows tooling and documentation depth. AgenticCareerBoost sits above both as a public system where source, state, reports, and checks have to stay aligned.
